master_row.title: Quantum Fields on Curved Manifolds
master_row.canonical_statement: On a Trawinistic manifold $M$, a quantum field is a section of an appropriate fibre bundle whose dynamics are given by covariant field equations using the Trawinistic connection; field operators satisfy canonical commutation relations adapted to the curved geometry.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a differential equation.
master_row.dictionary: On a Trawinistic manifold (M), a quantum field is a section of an appropriate fibre bundle whose dynamics are given by covariant field equations using the Trawinistic connection; field operators satisfy canonical commutation relations adapted to the curved geometry.
master_row.encyclopedia: Quantum fields on a Trawinistic manifold are geometric objects whose evolution is determined by TZP-compatible covariant differential structure. % CANONICAL PDF-ALIGNED VERSION Interpretive role: This entry opens the quantum-curved branch of the registry. It provides the base language needed for vacuum response, confined modes, Casimir corrections, and running couplings on a non-flat background.
